research_question: Can a mixed price-volume family built only from the retained atomic signals discovered in rr_price_volume_single_signal_discovery_20260422 produce a stronger and more robust edge than calibrated v7 without reintroducing rejected signals?

feature_set.interpretation: stronger medium-term momentum should rank higher
more internally consistent recent trend should rank higher
improving recent liquidity versus medium-term liquidity should rank higher

score_family_contract.score_rule: mean(percentile_rank(momentum_60_5_raw DESC), percentile_rank(trend_consistency_20d_raw DESC), percentile_rank(liquidity_trend_20_60_raw DESC)); require min_feature_count >= 2
score_family_contract.missing_data_rule: Only score rows with at least 2 non-null ranked features; otherwise emit null model_score_D0 and preserve the row for audit.

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same calibrated v7 0.70 ranking liquidity guard and same TopK=10 width
constants_frozen.portfolio_contract: same equal-weight daily Top10 extraction used by calibrated v7

disallowed_changes: Do not mix any rejected atomic signals from rr_price_volume_single_signal_discovery_20260422 into this family.
Do not include price_volume_single_signal_momentum_120_20_v1 in this first constructed family; treat it as borderline and leave it out unless separately promoted later.
Do not change the calibrated v7 liquidity guard threshold or guard stage.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not add extraction, weight-penalty, or turnover-suppression changes inside this round.
Do not sweep multiple family compositions or aggregation formulas inside this round.
notes: This is the first mixed family constructed only from retained atomic price-volume signals discovered under the discovery-round contract.
The family intentionally excludes all signals currently labeled signal_edge_negative and also excludes the borderline momentum_120_20 candidate.

The objective is to test whether combining one strength signal, one trend-quality signal, and one liquidity-improvement signal yields a cleaner gross edge than the earlier mixed price-volume attempts.
